prompt:
Create an original black-line-art medical instruction illustration in the universal patient-information-leaflet style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on pure white background, minimal short parallel hatching for soft shadow only, no other color except red blood inside a vial, no frame, no border, no captions, no labels, no logos, no numbers.

Subject — a DUAL-STATE composition arranged side-by-side in a 1:1 square. LEFT STATE on the left half, a curved black ARROW pointing right in the middle, RIGHT STATE on the right half.

LEFT STATE: an upright cylindrical WHITE PROTECTOR TUBE BODY (open at the top, waiting for a vial). The body is stubby pill-bottle proportions (height ≈ 1.1x its own diameter on its own). The body has the word 'HEALTH' embossed vertically along its side wall. The word 'HEALTH' is rendered as a vertical column of 6 RIGHT-SIDE-UP outlined capital letters stacked top-to-bottom along the body's vertical axis. Reading from the TOP of the body DOWN: H (topmost, just below the body's open mouth) → E → A → L → T → H (bottommost, near the body's base). Each letter upright, NOT upside-down, NOT mirrored. Thin black outline strokes — line-art embossed look, NOT solid black blocks. One hand from upper-left holds a tall narrow capped blood-filled glass vial (small ribbed plain black-line-art cap on top, transparent tube with red blood filling about 70% of the vial) and lowers the vial DOWN into the open mouth of the protector tube body. Lower portion of the vial sits inside the body's mouth; upper portion (with cap) extends above.

BETWEEN THE TWO STATES: a SOLID BLACK CURVED ARROW pointing right (from left state to right state), arching gently across the center of the composition.

RIGHT STATE: the ASSEMBLED upright protector tube — same WHITE BODY as in the left state with the same HEALTH text in the same orientation (vertical column of upright outlined capital letters, H at the top of the body, H at the bottom, reading top-down spells HEALTH forward). On TOP of the body sits a BLACK PROTECTOR CAP — a solid-black cylindrical cap of the same diameter as the body, cap height ≈ 0.5–0.6x the cap's diameter (short squat cap). The cap is filled solid black. The cap has the word 'DIALED' embossed vertically along its side wall — rendered as a vertical column of 6 RIGHT-SIDE-UP outlined capital letters stacked top-to-bottom along the cap's vertical axis. Reading from the TOP of the cap DOWN: D (topmost, near the very top of the cap) → I → A → L → E → D (bottommost, just above the cap/body seam). Each letter upright, NOT upside-down, NOT mirrored. Thin WHITE outline strokes against the solid-black cap (letter interior remains black) — line-art embossed look, NOT solid white blocks. One hand from upper-right pinches the cap between thumb and fingers, lowering it onto the body. The blood-filled vial is now seated INSIDE the body (visible faintly through the body's open mouth, just a hint of red below the cap).

Combined reading top-to-bottom of the assembled tube in the RIGHT state: BLACK CAP showing 'DIALED' forward (D-I-A-L-E-D) → WHITE BODY showing 'HEALTH' forward (H-E-A-L-T-H) = 'DIALED HEALTH' linearly top-down, both words correctly spelled forward, all letters right-side-up.

CRITICAL: the text orientation on the protector tube must EXACTLY match the text orientation shown in the FIRST provided reference image (the approved step 17). In the step 17 reference, the protector tube has the word DIALED on the cap with the first D at the top, and the word HEALTH on the body with the first H at the top — replicate this exact orientation on both the cap and body in your output.

The SECOND reference image is the real product photo (protector tube top.png) for confirming the assembled tube's appearance.

Style: uniform medium-weight black ink line work on pure white background, minimal short parallel hatching for soft shadow/volume on hands only, no other color except the red blood inside the vial in the left state. 1:1 square composition, generous clean white margins, no frame, no border, no caption, no labels, no logos, no numbers, no arrows other than the curved black arrow between the two states.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
